summary: No blocker or major issues. Task 10's systemInstruction plumbing and thinking accumulation are defensive: safe cast + takeIf{isNotBlank} correctly null-coalesces blank/missing/non-string SYSTEM_PROMPT_DEFAULT values (covered by SystemInstructionTest); thinking output flows through the existing SafeMarkdown + SafeUriHandler scheme whitelist (http/https only, lowercased per RFC 3986) — no new markdown/URI surface. Thinking accumulation is in-memory only, per-send StringBuilder, never logged or persisted. Two low-severity hardening observations: (1) thinkingSb has no size cap, (2) buildSystemInstruction has no length cap — both acceptable under the current trust model (local on-device LLM, bundled allowlist JSON) but worth flagging for Phase-3 when DataStore user overrides land.

findings.issue: thinkingSb StringBuilder accumulates partialThinking chunks with no upper bound. A model emitting very long reasoning (or a future broken/hostile engine build) could grow the buffer until OOM. In-memory only, not persisted, not logged — per-send lifecycle limits blast radius to one conversation turn.
findings.fix: Optional: add a soft cap (e.g. 256 KB) and stop appending once exceeded — `if (thinkingSb.length < MAX_THINKING_CHARS) thinkingSb.append(partialThinking)`. Acceptable to defer; flag in Phase-3 when remote / less-trusted models land.

findings.issue: buildSystemInstruction does not cap the length of the incoming systemPrompt string. Today the source is bundled allowlist JSON (trusted), but tech-spec D24 notes Phase-3 DataStore overrides will flow through the same configValues map. An attacker-controlled override with a megabyte-long prompt would be forwarded to litertlm verbatim.
findings.fix: When Phase-3 introduces user/DataStore overrides, add a `take(MAX_SYSTEM_PROMPT_CHARS)` (e.g. 8–16 KB) before wrapping in Content.Text. No change required now — flag as a TODO tied to the Phase-3 plumbing task.

owasp_items_checked: A01 Broken Access Control — n/a (no auth surface touched)
A02 Cryptographic Failures — n/a (no crypto in diff)
A03 Injection — systemInstruction wrapped in Content.Text (no template interpolation); thinking/answer rendered via CommonMark (no HTML/JS execution); SafeUriHandler blocks non-http(s) schemes
A04 Insecure Design — blank/null/non-string SYSTEM_PROMPT_DEFAULT correctly collapse to null; per-send thinkingSb isolation prevents cross-turn leak; two minor resource-exhaustion observations above
A05 Security Misconfiguration — SafeMarkdown scheme whitelist unchanged and still enforced by MessageBubble/ThinkingBlock composition paths
A06 Vulnerable Components — no dependency bumps in this commit
A08 Software and Data Integrity — no deserialization of untrusted input in diff; systemInstruction path uses typed cast, not reflection/JSON decode

A09 Security Logging and Monitoring — verified: no Log.* / errorLog calls emit thinkingText, partialThinking, systemInstruction, or prompt content; only errorLog.e("inference", safeMsg) on engine error (pre-existing)
A10 SSRF — n/a (on-device LLM, no server-side URL fetching introduced)
MASVS-STORAGE — thinkingText and systemInstruction live in memory only, not persisted
MASVS-PLATFORM — Intent.ACTION_VIEW is limited to http/https URIs via SafeUriHandler allowlist
Hardcoded secrets scan — none in diff (allowlist values are model metadata, not credentials)
